secondary_meaning: a person you work with in the same office or organization
usage_notes: Can be masculine or feminine. Common in professional contexts.
common_collocations: collègue de travail, mes collègues, bon collègue
image_context: Two colleagues working together at office desks
example_1: Mon collègue m'a aidée avec le projet.
example_1_translation: My colleague helped me with the project.
example_2: Elle est devenue mon amie et ma collègue.
example_2_translation: She became my friend and colleague.
memory_hook: Colleague + French = collègue. Someone you work alongside.

usage_notes: Always masculine. Used for monthly or annual payment.
common_collocations: salaire minimum, salaire annuel, augmentation de salaire
image_context: Paycheck or salary statement with money symbols
example_1: Elle reçoit son salaire à la fin du mois.
example_1_translation: She receives her salary at the end of the month.
example_2: Le salaire minimum a été augmenté.
example_2_translation: The minimum wage has been increased.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first: SA-laire. The 'ai' sounds like 'eh'.
memory_hook: Salaire = salary. Similar words in both languages!

usage_notes: Always feminine. Essential for business French.
common_collocations: petite entreprise, grande entreprise, créer une entreprise
image_context: Modern office building representing a business company
example_1: Cette entreprise compte deux cents employés.
example_1_translation: This company has two hundred employees.
example_2: Il a fondé sa propre entreprise.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on last: an-tre-PRI-ze. The 'i' sounds like 'ee'.
memory_hook: Entreprise = enterprise. Same meaning in both languages!
common_mistakes: Remember it's always feminine (l'entreprise, une entreprise)

secondary_meaning: sad or dissatisfied because something did not meet expectations
usage_notes: Changes form: déçu (masc), déçue (fem). Past participle of 'décevoir'.
common_collocations: très déçu, être déçu par, déçu de
image_context: Person with disappointed expression showing sadness
example_1: Je suis déçu par les résultats.
example_1_translation: I am disappointed with the results.
example_2: Elle était très déçue de ne pas avoir réussi.
example_2_translation: She was very disappointed to have failed.
example_3: Tu dois être déçu de ce film.
example_3_translation: You must be disappointed by this film.

secondary_meaning: feeling nervous or apprehensive about something
usage_notes: Changes form: anxieux (masc), anxieuse (fem). B2 level vocabulary.
common_collocations: très anxieux, être anxieux de, anxieux pour
image_context: Worried person with hands on head showing anxiety
example_1: Je suis anxieux avant les examens.
example_2: L'attente des résultats le rend anxieux.
example_2_translation: Waiting for the results makes him anxious.
example_3: Elle est anxieuse pour son entretien d'emploi.
example_3_translation: She is anxious about her job interview.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on last: an-ksieux. The 'ieux' rhymes with French 'mieux' (better).
memory_hook: Anxious in English → anxieux in French. Same root word.

secondary_meaning: feeling or showing keen interest and excitement
usage_notes: Same form for masculine and feminine. Can also be a noun.
common_collocations: très enthousiaste, être enthousiaste de, enthousiaste pour
image_context: Excited enthusiastic person with arms raised celebrating
example_1: Elle est très enthousiaste à l'idée de voyager.
example_1_translation: She is very enthusiastic about the idea of traveling.
example_2: L'équipe était enthousiaste pour le nouveau projet.
example_2_translation: The team was enthusiastic about the new project.
example_3: Il est enthousiaste d'apprendre le français.
example_3_translation: He is enthusiastic about learning French.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on last: an-too-zias-TE. The 'ia' sounds like 'ya'.
common_mistakes: Do not add 's' for feminine (same form)

secondary_meaning: a public gathering to show support or opposition
usage_notes: Always feminine. Important for news and politics vocabulary.
common_collocations: manifestation pacifique, manifestation politique, participer à une manifestation
image_context: Large crowd protest demonstration with signs
example_1: La manifestation pacifique a rassemblé mille personnes.
example_1_translation: The peaceful demonstration gathered a thousand people.
example_2: Les manifestants ont défilé dans les rues.
example_2_translation: The protesters marched through the streets.
example_3: La manifestation contre la guerre était importante.
example_3_translation: The demonstration against the war was significant.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on '-tation': man-i-fes-ta-SION. The 'tion' sounds like 'syohn'.
memory_hook: Manifestation = manifest + ation. A public display of opinion.
common_mistakes: Do not confuse with 'manifestant' (a protester, masculine noun)

secondary_meaning: a serious situation requiring immediate action
usage_notes: Always feminine. 'Urgences' refers to emergency room/services.
common_collocations: situation d'urgence, appeler les urgences, cas d'urgence
synonyms: situation d'urgence, cas grave, urgence médicale
image_context: Emergency room with medical equipment and urgency signs
example_3: Il y a une urgence à l'hôpital.
example_3_translation: There's an emergency at the hospital.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'ur-ZHANS'. The 'g' before 'e' sounds like 'zh' (soft).
memory_hook: Urgent + ence = urgence (the state of being urgent).
common_mistakes: Remember 'urgences' is the plural for emergency services

secondary_meaning: serious disagreement or argument between parties
usage_notes: Always masculine. Common in news and political discourse. B2 level.
common_collocations: conflit territorial, conflit interne, résoudre un conflit
image_context: Two opposing sides in conflict representing territorial dispute
example_2: Ils cherchent une solution au conflit.
example_2_translation: They are looking for a solution to the conflict.
example_3: Ce conflit a duré des années.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'kon-FLEE'. The 'li' rhymes with French 'lit' (bed).
memory_hook: Conflict in English → conflit in French. Similar pronunciation.

secondary_meaning: a sign or indication of a disease or medical condition
usage_notes: Always masculine. Common in medical French.
common_collocations: symptômes de grippe, symptômes graves, avoir des symptômes
image_context: Person with hand on forehead showing illness symptoms
example_2: Les symptômes ont disparu après une semaine.
example_2_translation: The symptoms disappeared after a week.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'sim-TOME'. The 'pt' is silent, pronounced like 'tom'.
memory_hook: Symptom → symptôme. Same word in both languages!
common_mistakes: The 'p' is silent: it sounds like 'sim-tome', not 'simp-tome'

secondary_meaning: medical care given for an illness or injury
usage_notes: Always masculine. B2 vocabulary for medical contexts.
common_collocations: traitement médical, suivi un traitement, traitement efficace
image_context: Medical treatment with pills and prescription
example_1: Le traitement est efficace mais coûteux.
example_1_translation: The treatment is effective but expensive.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first: TRAIT-ment. The 'ai' sounds like 'eh'.
memory_hook: Treatment → traitement. Medical care in French.
common_mistakes: Do not confuse with 'trait' (a line or feature)

secondary_meaning: a professional who treats patients and prescribes medicine
usage_notes: Can be masculine or feminine. Essential health vocabulary.
common_collocations: aller voir le médecin, médecin généraliste, médecin spécialiste
image_context: Doctor in white coat with stethoscope
example_1: Je vais voir le médecin demain.
example_1_translation: I am going to see the doctor tomorrow.
example_2: Le médecin m'a prescrit des médicaments.
example_2_translation: The doctor prescribed me some medicine.
common_mistakes: Do not confuse with 'médecine' (medicine, the field)

secondary_meaning: a certificate awarded by an educational institution
usage_notes: Always masculine. Common in academic and professional contexts.
common_collocations: obtenir un diplôme, diplôme universitaire, diplôme d'études
example_1: Elle a obtenu son diplôme avec mention.
example_1_translation: She obtained her diploma with honors.
example_2: Un diplôme universitaire est requis pour ce poste.
example_2_translation: A university degree is required for this position.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first: DIP-lôme. The 'ô' sounds like 'oh'.
memory_hook: Diploma → diplôme. Same word in both languages!

secondary_meaning: a formal test of knowledge or ability
usage_notes: Always masculine. Essential for academic French.
common_collocations: passer un examen, examen important, résultats d'examen
image_context: Student taking exam with pencil and test paper
example_1_translation: I have an important exam tomorrow.
example_2: Les résultats de l'examen seront publiés vendredi.
example_2_translation: The exam results will be published on Friday.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on last: é-gza-MEN. The 'xa' sounds like 'gza'.
memory_hook: Exam → examen. Same word in both languages!

secondary_meaning: a structured set of courses offered by an educational institution
usage_notes: Always masculine. B2 vocabulary for academic discussions.
common_collocations: curriculum scolaire, curriculum universitaire, modifier le curriculum
image_context: Books and syllabus representing educational curriculum
example_1: Le curriculum comprend des cours obligatoires.
example_2: Le nouveau curriculum a été approuvé.
example_2_translation: The new curriculum has been approved.
example_3: Le curriculum est très chargé cette année.
example_3_translation: The curriculum is very busy this year.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first: KY-ri-cu-lum. The 'u' in first syllable is like 'oo'.
memory_hook: Curriculum in English → curriculum in French (Latin origin).
